description: Keep the four Store destinations visible at one level instead of placing them in a menu.
user_or_business_purpose: Supports fast orientation and direct shelf switching without requiring shoppers to learn hidden structure.
observed_examples: NAMESAKE uses a direct browse layer; RTRFM keeps major destinations explicit despite a larger content system.
award_or_recognition: NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023; RTRFM: Webby Music winner 2025
suitable_context: Catalogs with roughly four to seven stable peer categories.
unsuitable_context: Large taxonomies needing multiple subcategory levels.
accessibility_considerations: Use a named nav landmark and native links; keep every destination keyboard reachable.
performance_considerations: Static HTML and CSS are sufficient.
recommended_application: Keep All, BlackBox Releases, Distro, and Merch as one visible peer set.

description: Provide an explicit All destination before narrower shelves.
user_or_business_purpose: Gives shoppers a predictable way to clear a category choice and recover the complete assortment.
observed_examples: Compact editorial and commerce rails such as Highsnobiety preserve broad entry points beside narrower topics.
award_or_recognition: Highsnobiety: Webby People's Voice winner 2024; NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023
suitable_context: Small catalogs where the combined collection remains useful.
unsuitable_context: Catalogs where All would create an unmanageably long or incoherent result.
accessibility_considerations: Label the link plainly and expose aria-current when active.
performance_considerations: No extra fetch or state is needed because All is already a route.
recommended_application: Retain All as the first category and make its active state unmistakable.

description: Place Store navigation within the label's cultural system rather than making it resemble a generic shop application.
user_or_business_purpose: Maintains label identity while keeping buying paths discoverable.
observed_examples: i-D and Highsnobiety place commerce beside editorial culture; Studio KARO merges art, clothing, music, and video.
award_or_recognition: i-D: Webby publication winner 2025; Highsnobiety: Webby People's Voice winner 2024; Studio KARO: CSSDA WOTD 2025
suitable_context: Independent labels where releases and editorial identity reinforce each other.
unsuitable_context: Commodity marketplaces where brand content is secondary to search and comparison.
accessibility_considerations: Do not let expressive styling obscure category names or current location.
performance_considerations: Reuse existing typography and tokens; avoid extra media in the header.
recommended_application: Style the Store rail as a label index using the existing Veneer and mono system.

description: Keep category choices distinct from cart, search, account, locale, and format filters.
user_or_business_purpose: Prevents competing tasks from diluting the main browse decision.
observed_examples: NAMESAKE separates browse from utilities; RTRFM separates contextual merch from portal utilities.
award_or_recognition: NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023; RTRFM: Webby Music winner 2025
suitable_context: Headers with one clear category task and utilities elsewhere in the shell.
unsuitable_context: Single-purpose landing pages with no recurring utilities.
accessibility_considerations: Use distinct landmark names when more than one nav is present.
performance_considerations: Avoid mounting additional interactive clients in the category component.
recommended_application: Keep the Store rail visually distinct from Distro format controls and the shell cart.

description: Use top and bottom rules to make the category zone read as a structural band without a floating card.
user_or_business_purpose: Creates a stable transition between masthead and catalog while preserving the site's raw editorial surface.
observed_examples: LNWY uses monochrome restraint; RCA and District use grid and rule logic as identity.
award_or_recognition: LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017; RCA: CSSDA Special Kudos 2023; District: CSSDA WOTD 2019
suitable_context: Dark editorial pages with aligned content containers.
unsuitable_context: Soft consumer interfaces whose visual language depends on rounded floating surfaces.
accessibility_considerations: Rules must retain sufficient contrast without becoming visual noise at zoom.
performance_considerations: Pure CSS borders add negligible cost.
recommended_application: Keep the existing border-y band and strengthen its internal layout.

description: Give each shelf an equal grid cell with a label, index, and optional count.
user_or_business_purpose: Improves scanning and gives every category equal perceived importance.
observed_examples: Musical Zoo uses repeatable grid rhythm; Crappy Explanation draws on record-store organization; NAMESAKE uses a strong commerce grid.
award_or_recognition: Musical Zoo: CSSDA WOTD 2018; Crappy Explanation: CSSDA Special Kudos 2021; NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023
suitable_context: Four stable categories with short labels.
unsuitable_context: Many categories or labels that vary greatly in length.
accessibility_considerations: Cells must remain real links with at least 44px height and clear focus treatment.
performance_considerations: CSS Grid only; no runtime layout code.
recommended_application: Use four desktop columns and two mobile columns.

description: Pair an oversized current-shelf heading with compact metadata and the category index.
user_or_business_purpose: Makes the page feel authored by a label while clarifying current context.
observed_examples: Analogue Foundation uses whitespace and editorial rhythm; i-D uses strong type hierarchy; RCA uses asymmetrical brutalist grids.
award_or_recognition: Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee 2024; i-D: Webby winner 2025; RCA: CSSDA Special Kudos 2023
suitable_context: Brand-led collection pages with concise descriptions.
unsuitable_context: Utility-heavy pages where vertical space is scarce.
accessibility_considerations: Heading order must stay logical; asymmetric placement cannot change reading order.
performance_considerations: Typography and grid only; avoid hero media.
recommended_application: Integrate Store title, current shelf, count, and rail in one bordered band for the masthead direction.

pattern_name: Visible reflow instead of hidden overflow
description: Recompose the category row at narrow widths so all destinations remain visible.
user_or_business_purpose: Avoids undiscoverable off-screen categories and unpredictable wrapping.
observed_examples: UPPERGROUND and The Cloister both received recognition with dedicated desktop and mobile compositions.
award_or_recognition: UPPERGROUND: Awwwards HM 2023; The Cloister: Awwwards HM 2023
unsuitable_context: Large carousels where horizontal exploration is itself the task.
accessibility_considerations: Meet WCAG reflow and preserve complete labels at 320 CSS px.
performance_considerations: Media queries and CSS Grid are sufficient.
recommended_application: Switch the record-bin direction to a two-by-two mobile ledger.

description: Make the selected collection name the clearest text after the Store label.
user_or_business_purpose: Helps shoppers verify location before scanning products.
observed_examples: i-D demonstrates compact navigation under strong typographic hierarchy; Analogue Foundation uses deliberate editorial emphasis.
award_or_recognition: i-D: Webby winner 2025; Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee 2024
suitable_context: Collection pages where route context matters.
unsuitable_context: A minimal rail-only treatment where the page hero already repeats the exact shelf title.
accessibility_considerations: Maintain one h1 and a logical heading hierarchy.
performance_considerations: Text emphasis has no material runtime cost.
recommended_application: In the masthead direction, promote the active category heading rather than repeating generic Store copy.

description: Add one short description or item count near the current shelf, not a paragraph of marketing copy.
user_or_business_purpose: Sets expectation without delaying access to products.
observed_examples: Apple Music 100 Best Albums and RTRFM use short orienting context before deeper catalogs.
award_or_recognition: Apple Music 100: Webby nominee 2025; RTRFM: Webby winner 2025
suitable_context: Small catalogs with meaningful shelf descriptions or counts.
unsuitable_context: Headers where copy would duplicate the next section verbatim.
accessibility_considerations: Keep the line readable at 200% zoom and do not encode status through position alone.
performance_considerations: Use build-time content and existing collection length.
recommended_application: Limit context to one sentence and one live count.

description: Place the Store identity or current-shelf context immediately above the category choices.
user_or_business_purpose: Frames the decision and prevents the rail from looking like detached site-wide navigation.
observed_examples: RCA and Because Recollection establish a branded frame before deeper catalog exploration.
award_or_recognition: RCA: CSSDA Special Kudos 2023; Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY 2016
suitable_context: Store pages that currently separate hero and navigation weakly.
unsuitable_context: Compact utility bars where context is already obvious from persistent chrome.
accessibility_considerations: DOM order should match visual order.
performance_considerations: No additional asset is necessary.
recommended_application: Keep Store/current shelf copy adjacent to the category component.

description: End the navigation band before product cards or discovery panels begin.
user_or_business_purpose: Separates deciding where to browse from evaluating products.
observed_examples: The Cloister and NAMESAKE use controlled whitespace and distinct browse/product zones.
award_or_recognition: The Cloister: Awwwards HM 2023; NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023
suitable_context: Catalog pages with repeated product cards.
unsuitable_context: Immersive narratives where navigation and content intentionally blend.
accessibility_considerations: Do not rely on whitespace alone; retain a structural boundary.
performance_considerations: Static spacing and border rules only.
recommended_application: Preserve a clear bottom rule and measured gap before Store shelf content.

description: Use anchors with href for every category instead of tabs, buttons, or scripted divs.
user_or_business_purpose: Preserves expected navigation, open-in-new-tab, copy-link, and no-JavaScript fallback behavior.
observed_examples: Awarded commerce and editorial references use destination navigation for browse layers; W3C explicitly recommends native links.
award_or_recognition: W3C APG guidance; NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023
unsuitable_context: Client-only views with no meaningful URL or history entry.
accessibility_considerations: Native anchors supply standard keyboard and assistive-technology behavior.
performance_considerations: Avoids client code and hydration.
recommended_application: Preserve the current anchor markup and app-shell interception.

description: Combine heavier text, a 2–3px rule, and a low-opacity surface for the current shelf.
user_or_business_purpose: Makes location easier to scan while retaining the raw monochrome system.
observed_examples: LNWY uses a restrained active marker; UPPERGROUND shows how one label colour can carry identity.
award_or_recognition: LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017; UPPERGROUND: Awwwards HM 2023
unsuitable_context: Dense multilevel navigation where large active surfaces compete with hierarchy.
accessibility_considerations: Do not use burgundy alone; aria-current must remain and focus needs a separate treatment.
performance_considerations: CSS colour and border transitions only.
recommended_application: Use the existing Store accent token with a non-colour rule and weight cue.

description: Prefix stable shelves with small 01–04 numbers.
user_or_business_purpose: Creates record-catalog character and improves left-to-right scanning without icons.
observed_examples: RCA, District, and Because Recollection use index and archive language as part of music discovery.
award_or_recognition: RCA: CSSDA Special Kudos 2023; District: CSSDA WOTD 2019; Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY 2016
suitable_context: A fixed four-item navigation where order is meaningful and stable.
unsuitable_context: Dynamic categories that frequently reorder or localize differently.
accessibility_considerations: Numbers are supplementary; accessible names should still foreground category labels.
performance_considerations: Plain text adds no meaningful cost.
recommended_application: Use 01–04 as quiet mono metadata in the record-crate direction.

description: Show a small derived item count when it helps users choose between shelves.
user_or_business_purpose: Sets assortment expectations before navigation and can reduce empty-route visits.
observed_examples: Record-store and archive metaphors in Because Recollection and Crappy Explanation support count-like catalog metadata.
award_or_recognition: Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY 2016; Crappy Explanation: CSSDA Special Kudos 2021
suitable_context: Small catalogs where counts are current and materially different.
unsuitable_context: Rapidly changing counts, very large numbers, or counts that add noise without helping choice.
accessibility_considerations: Do not announce count changes as live status unless they change in-session.
performance_considerations: Derive at build time from the already-loaded catalog; no extra request.
recommended_application: Treat counts as removable metadata in the record-crate PoC, not a requirement.

description: Pair the existing distressed display face for Store/current shelf with mono uppercase labels for navigation metadata.
user_or_business_purpose: Expresses underground-label character while keeping controls systematic.
observed_examples: RCA and District use bold type as music identity; i-D uses strong display hierarchy with compact navigation.
award_or_recognition: RCA: Communication Arts Webpick; District: CSSDA WOTD 2019; i-D: Webby winner 2025
suitable_context: Editorial mastheads and compact category systems.
unsuitable_context: Long body copy or very small controls using distressed display fonts.
accessibility_considerations: Keep decorative display type out of small labels and preserve readable fallback fonts.
performance_considerations: Reuse loaded Veneer/Bebas and Geist Mono; add no font request.
recommended_application: Use Veneer only for masthead hierarchy and Geist Mono for shelf links and metadata.

description: Use 12–13px minimum for tracked uppercase category labels rather than the current 11px.
user_or_business_purpose: Improves scanning on dark backgrounds without abandoning the label's utility typography.
observed_examples: LNWY and The Cloister show sparse systems where small labels have room to breathe.
award_or_recognition: LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017; The Cloister: Awwwards HM 2023
unsuitable_context: Dense legal, metadata, or multilingual labels that require mixed case.
accessibility_considerations: Test at zoom and on narrow screens; uppercase and tracking can reduce word-shape recognition.
performance_considerations: CSS only.
recommended_application: Raise category text one step while retaining the current mono treatment.

description: Reduce letter spacing as labels grow so BlackBox Releases stays compact and readable.
user_or_business_purpose: Maintains the industrial mono voice without creating awkward line length.
observed_examples: i-D and RCA demonstrate controlled type contrast rather than uniform extreme tracking.
award_or_recognition: i-D: Webby winner 2025; RCA: Communication Arts Webpick
unsuitable_context: Decorative poster headlines where exaggerated tracking is the composition.
accessibility_considerations: Avoid spacing that makes words hard to parse for low-vision and cognitive users.
performance_considerations: CSS variable or utility change only.
recommended_application: Tune tracking from 0.18em toward roughly 0.12–0.16em after visual testing.

description: Limit the header to one display level and one utility level.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps the component authored but calm and prevents an award-site aesthetic from becoming a type sampler.
observed_examples: Analogue Foundation and LNWY rely on restraint; The Music Project shows the risk of turning texture and novelty into the main interface.
award_or_recognition: Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee 2024; LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017; The Music Project: CSSDA Special Kudos 2025
unsuitable_context: Long editorial feature pages needing a broader scale.
accessibility_considerations: Ensure labels do not depend on stylized glyphs for meaning.
performance_considerations: Reuses existing font files and reduces style complexity.
recommended_application: Do not introduce a third typeface, icon font, or decorative lettering style.

description: Use one existing burgundy accent as a small active-state hit against the monochrome base.
user_or_business_purpose: Adds Store-specific identity without weakening the label's dark visual continuity.
observed_examples: UPPERGROUND uses a concentrated label palette; underground poster systems often gain force from one registration colour rather than many.
award_or_recognition: UPPERGROUND: Awwwards HM 2023; Make It Rain Records: CSSDA WOTD 2018
suitable_context: Active rules, registration marks, and tiny metadata accents.
unsuitable_context: Large text areas or broad backgrounds where burgundy contrast is insufficient.
accessibility_considerations: Verify accent contrast and pair it with border/weight changes.
performance_considerations: Existing CSS tokens; no asset.
recommended_application: Use --store-accent and --store-accent-surface only on active or focused Store elements.

description: Keep off-black, near-white, muted grey, and border grey as the main system.
user_or_business_purpose: Preserves BlackBox's established underground tone and lets artwork remain the loudest colour source.
observed_examples: LNWY is explicitly black and white; Analogue Foundation uses controlled material restraint.
award_or_recognition: LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017; Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee 2024
suitable_context: Label stores with visually varied cover art.
unsuitable_context: Brand systems whose identity requires multiple primary colours.
accessibility_considerations: Muted text must still meet contrast requirements at small sizes.
performance_considerations: No new colour assets or filters.
recommended_application: Keep the rail neutral until hover, focus, or current state.

description: Differentiate the active cell with a subtle tint, not gradient, glow, glass, or shadow.
user_or_business_purpose: Creates state depth while retaining a print/editorial rather than club-tech or luxury feel.
observed_examples: The Cloister and NAMESAKE use controlled commerce surfaces; Subterranean Dreams shows immersive colour that should not transfer to a utility control.
award_or_recognition: The Cloister: Awwwards HM 2023; NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023; Subterranean Dreams: CSSDA Special Kudos 2024
suitable_context: Square category cells on an off-black page.
unsuitable_context: Interfaces where elevation conveys actual layering or modality.
accessibility_considerations: Surface tint cannot be the only current-state cue.
performance_considerations: One rgba background declaration.
recommended_application: Apply the existing 14% Store accent surface to the current cell only.

description: Use foreground for current labels, muted foreground for inactive labels, and a quieter tone for index/count metadata.
user_or_business_purpose: Clarifies priority without adding more colours.
observed_examples: i-D and LNWY use typographic contrast inside limited palettes.
award_or_recognition: i-D: Webby winner 2025; LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017
unsuitable_context: Very small text where the third tone would fail contrast.
accessibility_considerations: Metadata still needs readable contrast; do not fade below legibility for style.
performance_considerations: CSS variables already exist.
recommended_application: Use foreground, muted-foreground, and a tested metadata opacity; remove metadata if contrast becomes marginal.

description: Keep every category link at least 44px high even when the visible text is compact.
user_or_business_purpose: Makes the rail comfortable for touch and imprecise pointing while preserving visual restraint.
observed_examples: Recognized responsive references such as UPPERGROUND and The Cloister show dedicated mobile handling; W3C documents minimum target sizing.
award_or_recognition: WCAG 2.2 understanding guidance; UPPERGROUND and The Cloister: Awwwards HM
suitable_context: All desktop and mobile shelf links.
unsuitable_context: Inline prose links where target height follows line height.
accessibility_considerations: Preserve or exceed the existing min-h-11 target.
performance_considerations: No performance impact.
recommended_application: Keep min-h-11 or increase cell height while centering label content.

description: Use one repeatable horizontal and vertical inset across all shelf cells.
user_or_business_purpose: Creates record-bin rhythm and prevents the long BlackBox Releases label from feeling cramped.
observed_examples: Musical Zoo and RCA use repeatable grid spacing as part of the composition.
award_or_recognition: Musical Zoo: CSSDA WOTD 2018; RCA: CSSDA Special Kudos 2023
unsuitable_context: Freeform collage layouts where deliberate misalignment is the primary idea.
accessibility_considerations: Allow text wrapping without clipping at zoom.
performance_considerations: CSS Grid gap and padding only.
recommended_application: Use roughly 16–20px mobile inset and 20–24px desktop inset.

description: Use measured empty space around the rail instead of adding decorative containers.
user_or_business_purpose: Makes a small catalog feel intentional and curated rather than underfilled.
observed_examples: Analogue Foundation, LNWY, and The Cloister use restraint and breathing room as part of brand expression.
award_or_recognition: Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee 2024; LNWY and The Cloister: Awwwards HM
unsuitable_context: Dense operational interfaces or long category lists.
accessibility_considerations: Whitespace must not separate labels from their controls or hide focus boundaries.
performance_considerations: No additional resource cost.
recommended_application: Let the rule-bounded band breathe; do not wrap it in another card.

description: Allow at most one index and one count per category; remove counts before shrinking text.
user_or_business_purpose: Protects clarity and keeps the underground reference from becoming decorative clutter.
observed_examples: RCA and The Music Project show high-expression systems whose novelty would overwhelm a four-choice utility if copied wholesale.
award_or_recognition: RCA: Communication Arts Webpick; The Music Project: CSSDA Special Kudos 2025
unsuitable_context: Analytics dashboards or archival catalogs where metadata is the core task.
accessibility_considerations: Never reduce label size or contrast to preserve optional metadata.
performance_considerations: Less DOM and CSS is preferable.
recommended_application: Prototype counts, but make their deletion the first simplification if the rail feels busy.

description: Keep markup, state styling, category order, and URL generation in the existing StoreCategoryNavigation component.
user_or_business_purpose: Prevents route variants from drifting and keeps the visual rule change localized.
observed_examples: Awarded systems such as RCA and RTRFM depend on repeatable content modules rather than page-specific navigation copies.
award_or_recognition: RCA: Communication Arts Webpick; RTRFM: Webby winner 2025
unsuitable_context: A future product where categories behave differently per route.
accessibility_considerations: Centralization should preserve nav semantics and aria-current.
performance_considerations: One server-rendered component; no hydration.
recommended_application: Change the existing component rather than adding a new navigation abstraction.

description: Render each visual cell from the existing category registry rather than hard-coding four blocks.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps conditional Merch behavior and route contracts intact.
observed_examples: NAMESAKE and Studio KARO demonstrate systematic commerce presentations across changing content.
award_or_recognition: NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023; Studio KARO: CSSDA WOTD 2025
suitable_context: Existing category map with stable ids and labels.
unsuitable_context: One-off campaign pages with intentionally bespoke links.
accessibility_considerations: Generated link text and current state must remain complete and correct.
performance_considerations: Build-time iteration only.
recommended_application: Continue mapping getDiscoverableStoreCatalogCategories and add visual metadata inside each map result.

description: If counts are shown, calculate them from the same collection entries already used to discover categories.
user_or_business_purpose: Avoids stale editorial numbers and duplicate content ownership.
observed_examples: Because Recollection and archive-led music sites make catalog metadata feel authoritative because it belongs to the catalog system.
award_or_recognition: Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY 2016; Apple Music 100: Webby nominee 2025
suitable_context: Static catalogs where entries are already loaded.
unsuitable_context: Counts requiring a separate runtime service or eventually consistent search index.
accessibility_considerations: Counts should not be announced as dynamic changes when they are static page content.
performance_considerations: Reuse existing build-time data; do not add API calls.
recommended_application: Only include counts if they fall out of listStoreCollectionEntries without extra queries.

description: Use the existing Store accent and shared border/foreground tokens instead of adding a new palette or component theme.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps Store distinct but consistent with the site design system.
observed_examples: UPPERGROUND demonstrates label colour used systematically; LNWY demonstrates the value of a narrow palette.
award_or_recognition: UPPERGROUND and LNWY: Awwwards HM
suitable_context: State styling within the current BlackBox Store.
unsuitable_context: A complete rebrand with an approved new colour system.
accessibility_considerations: Token combinations still require contrast checks in each state.
performance_considerations: Zero new asset or dependency cost.
recommended_application: Use --store-accent, --store-accent-surface, --border, --foreground, and --muted-foreground only.

description: Do not add a search field to solve a four-choice category problem.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps the header focused and avoids an input whose result model does not exist.
observed_examples: RTRFM and Highsnobiety justify search through far larger content sets; BlackBox does not share that scale.
award_or_recognition: RTRFM: Webby winner 2025; Highsnobiety: Webby People's Voice winner 2024
unsuitable_context: Future catalogs large enough that category browsing no longer finds known items.
accessibility_considerations: Avoids adding labeling, clear-button, error, and keyboard requirements unnecessarily.
performance_considerations: Avoids client state, indexing, and request cost.
recommended_application: Keep search out of this change.

pattern_name: No select as primary mobile navigation
description: Do not collapse the four shelves into a native or custom dropdown on mobile.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps all destinations visible and removes an extra open/select step.
observed_examples: UPPERGROUND and The Cloister show composed mobile layouts rather than treating mobile as a hidden desktop menu.
award_or_recognition: UPPERGROUND and The Cloister: Awwwards HM
suitable_context: Four short destinations that fit in a two-column ledger.
unsuitable_context: Very large destination sets where visible links consume excessive height.
accessibility_considerations: Visible links are easier to scan and preserve native link semantics.
performance_considerations: Avoids JavaScript and custom-select code.
recommended_application: Use a two-by-two grid rather than a category select.

pattern_name: Search remains a separate future control
description: If Store search is later justified, place it beside or below the category system with an explicit label and independent state.
user_or_business_purpose: Prevents browse categories and keyword search from becoming one ambiguous control.
observed_examples: RTRFM uses scoped search within a larger portal; Splice separates editorial taxonomy from search.
award_or_recognition: RTRFM: Webby winner 2025; Splice Blog: Webby nominee 2025
suitable_context: A later catalog with validated search need.
unsuitable_context: This header improvement, which has no search requirement.
accessibility_considerations: Future input needs visible label, instructions, empty state, and keyboard behavior.
performance_considerations: Would require indexing and client or server query work; defer until needed.
recommended_application: Record this as excluded scope, not implementation work.

pattern_name: Links do not imitate filter toggles
description: Style category destinations as index cells or a rail, not as checkbox chips or pill toggles.
user_or_business_purpose: Communicates that each choice changes route and page context rather than mutating a local filter.
observed_examples: NAMESAKE uses browse navigation distinct from controls; BlackBox Distro already owns button-like format filtering.
award_or_recognition: NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023
unsuitable_context: In-page multi-select filtering with combinable values.
accessibility_considerations: Native link semantics and aria-current are more appropriate than pressed/selected button states.
performance_considerations: No control-state JavaScript.
recommended_application: Avoid pills and preserve square destination treatment.

description: On hover, strengthen label and rule before navigation without moving layout.
user_or_business_purpose: Signals clickability and previews the active visual language.
observed_examples: District and RCA use typographic interaction; their more expressive motion can be reduced to colour and border feedback here.
award_or_recognition: District: CSSDA WOTD 2019; RCA: Communication Arts Webpick
suitable_context: Pointer devices on the Store rail.
unsuitable_context: Touch-only assumptions or motion-heavy hover effects.
accessibility_considerations: Hover cannot be the only way to reveal meaning.
performance_considerations: Transition colour/border only.
recommended_application: Use hover text and border contrast without translation or scale.

description: Give keyboard focus a visible ring or outline that is distinct from current and hover states.
user_or_business_purpose: Lets keyboard users locate focus even when the focused link is already current.
observed_examples: W3C focus guidance requires sufficient visible indication; award-site minimalism should not erase it.
award_or_recognition: W3C WCAG guidance; LNWY: Awwwards HM
unsuitable_context: No exception for this component.
accessibility_considerations: Retain the global focus-visible treatment and test it on burgundy tint.
performance_considerations: CSS only.
recommended_application: Do not replace the repository focus ring with only an underline.

description: The active shelf stays visually marked after navigation and is programmatically exposed with aria-current.
user_or_business_purpose: Supports orientation across shell-managed page swaps and direct loads.
observed_examples: Compact browse systems in NAMESAKE and Highsnobiety maintain route context; W3C documents aria-current for navigation.
award_or_recognition: W3C technique; NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD 2023; Highsnobiety: Webby People's Voice winner 2024
unsuitable_context: Transient filters with no current-page meaning.
accessibility_considerations: Keep exactly one aria-current page value in the set.
performance_considerations: Server-rendered state; no runtime store.
recommended_application: Preserve current activeCategoryId logic and strengthen only presentation.

description: During shell navigation, keep the rail dimensions and current marker stable until the next page replaces content.
user_or_business_purpose: Avoids layout jump and uncertain state during short route transitions.
observed_examples: A Light in the Woods uses deliberate transition language; BlackBox should reduce that idea to continuity rather than spectacle.
award_or_recognition: A Light in the Woods: Webby nominee and People's Voice visual-design winner 2024
unsuitable_context: Long async filters that need explicit per-control progress.
accessibility_considerations: Do not disable native links or trap focus during navigation.
performance_considerations: Reuse the shell transition; add no component loader.
recommended_application: Leave loading ownership in AppShellRoot and avoid a new nav pending state.

description: Animate current/hover feedback through colour, background, and border over roughly 150–200ms.
user_or_business_purpose: Adds polish without turning a utility header into an attraction.
observed_examples: District, RCA, and UPPERGROUND use expressive interaction; the transferable minimum is immediate state confirmation.
award_or_recognition: District: CSSDA WOTD 2019; RCA: Communication Arts Webpick; UPPERGROUND: Awwwards HM 2023
unsuitable_context: Narrative hero sequences or deliberate immersive transitions.
accessibility_considerations: Respect reduced-motion preferences even though colour changes are low motion.
performance_considerations: Composite-free colour transitions are cheap at this scale.
recommended_application: Keep transition-colors and add no transforms.

description: Do not move, scale, or resize the active cell when routes change.
user_or_business_purpose: Preserves scan position and prevents neighboring labels from jumping.
observed_examples: LNWY's restrained monochrome system is the better model for this utility than sound-reactive District or RCA effects.
award_or_recognition: LNWY: Awwwards HM; District: CSSDA WOTD; RCA: Communication Arts Webpick
unsuitable_context: Expressive campaign components where movement itself communicates meaning.
accessibility_considerations: Layout stability helps magnification and cognitive tracking.
performance_considerations: Avoids layout and paint work from transforms or size changes.
recommended_application: Keep cell size identical in all states.

description: Any decorative transition should become immediate when prefers-reduced-motion is active.
user_or_business_purpose: Respects user preference without changing information or navigation.
observed_examples: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame exposes a Reduce Motion control; W3C treats interaction-triggered animation as an accessibility concern.
award_or_recognition: Rock Hall: Webby nominee 2026; W3C WCAG guidance
suitable_context: Any nonessential transition added to the Store rail.
unsuitable_context: Instant state changes with no motion already present.
accessibility_considerations: Motion preference must not remove current, hover, or focus information.
performance_considerations: A media query is sufficient.
recommended_application: Rely on existing global reduced-motion policy or add a scoped zero-duration override only if needed.

pattern_name: No sound, WebGL, or scroll theatre
description: Reject audio-reactive type, autoplay sound, 3D objects, and scroll hijacking for this header.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps shopping fast and avoids copying the distinctive spectacles of award sites.
observed_examples: District links audio to animation; Because Recollection and The Music Project use WebGL/3D; these are identity experiences, not category-control patterns.
award_or_recognition: District: CSSDA WOTD; Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY; The Music Project: CSSDA Special Kudos
unsuitable_context: Dedicated immersive music stories where exploration, not transaction, is the goal.
accessibility_considerations: Avoids vestibular, autoplay, and unexpected-audio barriers.
performance_considerations: Avoids the largest script, GPU, media, and network costs.
recommended_application: List these techniques as explicit OpenSpec non-goals.

category: Loading, empty, success, and error states
description: Do not render a category destination when its content type is intentionally undiscoverable and empty.
user_or_business_purpose: Prevents sending shoppers to a dead shelf.
observed_examples: Telepathic Instruments makes availability explicit; curated stores such as The Cloister avoid presenting irrelevant inventory doors.
award_or_recognition: Telepathic Instruments: Webby nominee 2025; The Cloister: Awwwards HM 2023
unsuitable_context: Categories that should remain visible with an intentional coming-soon state.
accessibility_considerations: Do not change category order unexpectedly within a session; route-level state should be clear.
performance_considerations: Build-time discovery; no request.
recommended_application: Preserve getDiscoverableStoreCatalogCategories behavior.

category: Loading, empty, success, and error states
pattern_name: Empty collection message after persistent navigation
description: If a reachable shelf has no products, keep the header and show a plain status message in the catalog region.
user_or_business_purpose: Preserves escape routes and explains the state without turning the header into an error panel.
observed_examples: Telepathic Instruments uses explicit sold-out language; independent stores keep browsing structure available around availability states.
award_or_recognition: Telepathic Instruments: Webby nominee 2025; Studio KARO: CSSDA WOTD 2025
unsuitable_context: Invalid routes, which need a not-found response.
accessibility_considerations: Use role=status for the empty message without auto-focusing it.
performance_considerations: Static text only.
recommended_application: Leave empty-state ownership in StoreCollectionPage and keep the category rail intact.

category: Loading, empty, success, and error states
description: Do not replace category labels with skeletons during shell fetches.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps orientation and lets the shopper retry or choose another shelf.
observed_examples: A Light in the Woods shows transition continuity; the BlackBox application shell already owns route loading globally.
award_or_recognition: A Light in the Woods: Webby nominee and visual-design People's Voice winner 2024
unsuitable_context: Data grids whose controls themselves depend on unknown server state.
accessibility_considerations: Persistent native links remain usable and understandable.
performance_considerations: Avoids duplicate skeleton markup and hydration.
recommended_application: Keep category HTML static across loading states.

category: Loading, empty, success, and error states
description: Because every category is a real href, failed shell interception can fall back to normal document navigation.
user_or_business_purpose: Provides a resilient path when enhanced navigation fails.
observed_examples: W3C recommends native links; recognized editorial sites retain URL-addressable destinations despite rich transitions.
award_or_recognition: W3C APG guidance; A Light in the Woods: Webby recognition 2024
unsuitable_context: Controls that perform mutations rather than navigation.
accessibility_considerations: Native links retain keyboard and assistive-technology behavior on failure.
performance_considerations: Progressive enhancement avoids duplicate error UI.
recommended_application: Do not replace href navigation with client-only state.

description: Wrap the shelf links in nav with an accessible name specific to Store categories.
user_or_business_purpose: Lets screen-reader users distinguish this navigation from the site header, Distro formats, and footer.
observed_examples: W3C guidance recommends purpose-based names such as Product; BlackBox already uses Store categories.
award_or_recognition: W3C APG guidance
unsuitable_context: A page with only one obvious navigation region, where a label is optional.
accessibility_considerations: Keep aria-label concise and unique among non-identical nav landmarks.
performance_considerations: No cost.
recommended_application: Preserve aria-label='Store categories'.

description: Use aria-current='page' on exactly the active category link.
user_or_business_purpose: Communicates the same location information conveyed visually.
observed_examples: W3C ARIA26 documents this technique for navigation bars.
award_or_recognition: W3C WCAG technique
unsuitable_context: Multi-select filters or tabs that do not represent current page.
accessibility_considerations: Exactly one item should carry the current page value.
performance_considerations: No cost.
recommended_application: Preserve the current activeCategoryId mapping.

description: Ensure each shelf target contains at least a 24px square and preferably retains the existing 44px height.
user_or_business_purpose: Reduces accidental activation for touch and motor-impaired users.
observed_examples: W3C WCAG 2.2 explains target size and spacing requirements.
award_or_recognition: W3C WCAG 2.2 understanding guidance
unsuitable_context: Inline links embedded in prose, which have a specific exception.
accessibility_considerations: Test wrapped labels and adjacent two-row mobile cells for sufficient target geometry.
performance_considerations: No material cost.
recommended_application: Keep min-h-11 and full-cell clickable area.

pattern_name: State never conveyed by colour alone
description: Pair burgundy with rule thickness, text weight, and aria-current.
user_or_business_purpose: Makes active location perceivable with colour-vision differences and monochrome displays.
observed_examples: W3C Use of Color guidance applies; LNWY shows a monochrome system can retain clear state.
award_or_recognition: W3C WCAG guidance; LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017
suitable_context: Any accented current or focus state.
unsuitable_context: Purely decorative colour changes with no informational meaning.
accessibility_considerations: Test current, hover, and focus independently.
performance_considerations: CSS only.
recommended_application: Use burgundy as reinforcement, not the sole marker.

description: Implement layout and states with server-rendered HTML and CSS only.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps category switching responsive and avoids adding maintenance for a static interaction.
observed_examples: LNWY's sparse navigation and Analogue Foundation's restraint transfer without their heavier page-level experiences.
award_or_recognition: LNWY: Awwwards HM 2017; Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee 2024
unsuitable_context: Controls needing live client-side selection, drag, or async data.
accessibility_considerations: Native semantics remain intact without hydration races.
performance_considerations: No new JavaScript bundle or hydration.
recommended_application: Use Astro markup and utility/global CSS only.

description: Do not add photos, video, texture images, canvases, or 3D to the category header.
user_or_business_purpose: Protects first-view speed and keeps product artwork as the visual focus.
observed_examples: Because Recollection, The Music Project, and Subterranean Dreams use media for immersive purposes that do not transfer to a utility header.
award_or_recognition: Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY; The Music Project and Subterranean Dreams: CSSDA Special Kudos
unsuitable_context: Dedicated campaign or listening experiences where media is primary content.
accessibility_considerations: Avoids autoplay and text-over-image contrast problems.
performance_considerations: Zero additional bytes and decode work.
recommended_application: Express the underground aesthetic with type, spacing, rules, and existing colour tokens.

description: Use Veneer/Bebas and Geist Mono already present in the page rather than introducing an award-inspired typeface.
user_or_business_purpose: Preserves brand consistency and avoids another font request or layout shift.
observed_examples: RCA and i-D show that typography can define identity, but their exact type systems are distinctive assets and should not be copied.
award_or_recognition: RCA: Communication Arts Webpick; i-D: Webby winner 2025
unsuitable_context: An approved full rebrand with budget for new font licensing and loading.
accessibility_considerations: Existing fallbacks and readable utility type remain available.
performance_considerations: No new font files, preloads, or CLS risk.
recommended_application: Limit PoCs and plan to existing font variables.

pattern_name: No extra catalog request for header metadata
description: Any count or category visibility must derive from existing build-time collection data.
user_or_business_purpose: Prevents a decorative header enhancement from delaying navigation or adding failure modes.
observed_examples: Archive-led references support rich metadata, but BlackBox can obtain it from its static catalog rather than a runtime service.
award_or_recognition: Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY 2016; Crappy Explanation: CSSDA Special Kudos 2021
suitable_context: Static Astro builds with existing entry lists.
unsuitable_context: Live inventory authority that must be real-time and cannot be represented safely at build time.
accessibility_considerations: Do not present static counts as live stock.
performance_considerations: No new API call; minimal build-time calculation.
recommended_application: Counts, if selected, represent catalog entries only and never inventory.

description: Render four category cells in two columns on narrow screens.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps every shelf visible within a compact height and gives labels generous target area.
observed_examples: The Cloister and UPPERGROUND explicitly present mobile variants; Musical Zoo demonstrates grid rhythm.
award_or_recognition: The Cloister and UPPERGROUND: Awwwards HM; Musical Zoo: CSSDA WOTD
unsuitable_context: Frequently changing category counts or very long localized labels.
accessibility_considerations: Maintain reading order 01–04 and full-cell focus indication.
performance_considerations: One media query.
recommended_application: Use grid-cols-2 on mobile and grid-cols-4 at desktop for the record-crate direction.

description: Allow BlackBox Releases to wrap naturally rather than abbreviating or clipping it.
user_or_business_purpose: Preserves category meaning and avoids forcing users to infer shorthand.
observed_examples: i-D and LNWY demonstrate compact labels with deliberate space rather than app-style truncation.
award_or_recognition: i-D: Webby winner 2025; LNWY: Awwwards HM
suitable_context: A two-column ledger with controlled cell padding.
unsuitable_context: Single-line toolbars where vertical expansion would obstruct a primary task.
accessibility_considerations: Text must remain legible under zoom and user font overrides.
performance_considerations: No cost.
recommended_application: Permit two lines in the long cell and keep equal minimum height.

description: Prefer visible grid reflow over an off-screen category rail.
user_or_business_purpose: Avoids hiding Merch or requiring an overflow cue that the current design lacks.
observed_examples: Recognized mobile compositions in The Cloister and UPPERGROUND support deliberate reflow.
award_or_recognition: The Cloister and UPPERGROUND: Awwwards HM
suitable_context: Four categories that fit a two-row grid.
unsuitable_context: Large swipeable media or timelines where horizontal movement is expected.
accessibility_considerations: WCAG reflow and keyboard traversal are simpler when all links are visible.
performance_considerations: Avoids scroll snapping and overflow scripts.
recommended_application: Do not implement horizontal scrolling for the chosen PoC unless user testing rejects the grid.

description: On mobile, cap the integrated masthead to one title, one short context line, and the category ledger.
user_or_business_purpose: Prevents the strongest visual direction from pushing products too far below the fold.
observed_examples: Analogue Foundation uses intentional pacing; Hellfest shows how event-scale content density would be excessive for a Store header.
award_or_recognition: Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee; Hellfest: Awwwards HM
unsuitable_context: Long-form editorial intros where reading is the page's primary task.
accessibility_considerations: Avoid fixed heights that clip zoomed or wrapped text.
performance_considerations: Less content improves initial paint and reduces layout height.
recommended_application: Use one sentence maximum and no mobile-only decorative media.

description: Use square or near-square cells and existing thin borders.
user_or_business_purpose: Connects the header to record sleeves, crates, print grids, and current Store cards.
observed_examples: RCA, Musical Zoo, Crappy Explanation, and NAMESAKE all support grid-led rather than soft-pill expression.
award_or_recognition: RCA: CSSDA Special Kudos; Musical Zoo and NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD; Crappy Explanation: CSSDA Special Kudos
unsuitable_context: Tag filters or status chips whose established component language is pill-based.
accessibility_considerations: Full-cell boundaries can help target perception; focus still needs its own indicator.
performance_considerations: Border CSS only.
recommended_application: Avoid rounded tabs and align cells to the catalog card geometry.

pattern_name: Hairline grid with thick active registration
description: Use 1px structural rules and one 3px active edge.
user_or_business_purpose: Creates a print-registration feel and a strong current marker without shadows.
observed_examples: LNWY uses thin monochrome markers; RCA and District use bold grid accents.
award_or_recognition: LNWY: Awwwards HM; RCA: CSSDA Special Kudos; District: CSSDA WOTD
unsuitable_context: Soft surfaces where heavy rules conflict with the design system.
accessibility_considerations: Active edge must not be the only state cue.
performance_considerations: Negligible CSS cost.
recommended_application: Use border-border for the grid and Store burgundy for the active top edge.

description: Separate the header through rules, spacing, and tint rather than box shadow.
user_or_business_purpose: Maintains a flat printed/editorial character and avoids generic card styling.
observed_examples: The Cloister and LNWY use restrained surfaces; i-D relies on typography rather than elevated panels.
award_or_recognition: The Cloister and LNWY: Awwwards HM; i-D: Webby winner
suitable_context: Category bands on the base page surface.
unsuitable_context: Modals, popovers, or genuinely layered controls.
accessibility_considerations: Flat boundaries still need sufficient contrast.
performance_considerations: Avoids shadow paint and CSS clutter.
recommended_application: Do not add shadow, glow, glass, or gradient to the Store rail.

pattern_name: Optional print imperfection as a tiny detail
description: If extra character is needed after the structural design works, use a restrained registration offset or low-opacity texture on one nonessential mark.
user_or_business_purpose: Adds underground print character without competing with cover art or compromising navigation.
observed_examples: The Music Project, Crappy Explanation, and Perpetuum.inc use tactile or retro visual language at larger scale.
award_or_recognition: The Music Project and Crappy Explanation: CSSDA Special Kudos; Perpetuum.inc: Awwwards HM
suitable_context: A later polish pass after hierarchy and accessibility are validated.
unsuitable_context: The first implementation or any detail that affects text legibility.
accessibility_considerations: Texture must be decorative, ignored by assistive tech, and removable under forced colours.
performance_considerations: Prefer CSS; do not add an image asset until a measured need exists.
recommended_application: Exclude texture from the initial OpenSpec slice; retain one burgundy registration mark as the cheap substitute.

description: Use sequence numbers, cells, and catalog counts to evoke physical record browsing without illustrating a crate.
user_or_business_purpose: Makes the Store feel native to an underground label using functional structure rather than themed decoration.
observed_examples: Because Recollection, Crappy Explanation, and LNWY connect music discovery to archive or record-store language.
award_or_recognition: Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY; Crappy Explanation: CSSDA Special Kudos; LNWY: Awwwards HM
unsuitable_context: Digital-only services where physical-media language would be misleading.
accessibility_considerations: Labels remain literal and understandable; the metaphor is supplementary.
performance_considerations: Text and CSS Grid only.
recommended_application: Use the record-crate direction as the leading PoC.

description: Use oversized distressed display type, mono metadata, asymmetric alignment, and rules like an underground gig poster.
user_or_business_purpose: Strengthens label voice while preserving a functional category path.
observed_examples: Hellfest, RCA, District, and i-D use bold type and subculture hierarchy in different forms.
award_or_recognition: Hellfest: Awwwards HM; RCA: Communication Arts Webpick; District: CSSDA WOTD; i-D: Webby winner
unsuitable_context: Tiny utility bars or pages already dominated by a large hero.
accessibility_considerations: Visual asymmetry cannot alter reading and focus order.
performance_considerations: Existing fonts and CSS only.
recommended_application: Use this only in the masthead PoC, with a strict mobile height budget.

description: Treat Store burgundy like a stamped mark used only for current state or one registration detail.
user_or_business_purpose: Creates a recognizable Store signature while leaving the broader site monochrome.
observed_examples: UPPERGROUND demonstrates a controlled label colour; underground print references support concentrated ink hits.
award_or_recognition: UPPERGROUND: Awwwards HM; Make It Rain Records: CSSDA WOTD
suitable_context: Active rules, small labels, and one decorative registration mark.
unsuitable_context: Large backgrounds, body copy, or multiple competing accents.
accessibility_considerations: Pair with non-colour state cues and verify contrast.
performance_considerations: Existing token only.
recommended_application: Use burgundy in less than roughly ten percent of the header area.

description: Use one direct line in BlackBox's voice instead of generic ecommerce slogans.
user_or_business_purpose: Reinforces curation and independence without needing photography or campaign assets.
observed_examples: RTRFM uses a repeated brand phrase; Analogue Foundation and i-D rely on editorial voice and pacing.
award_or_recognition: RTRFM: Webby winner; Analogue Foundation: Webby nominee; i-D: Webby winner
suitable_context: Integrated masthead with existing category descriptions.
unsuitable_context: Rail-only option where additional copy would duplicate the page below.
accessibility_considerations: Use plain language and avoid all-caps paragraphs.
performance_considerations: Static content only.
recommended_application: Reuse or tighten existing category description; do not invent campaign copy in implementation.

description: Keep every shelf one activation away from the current page.
user_or_business_purpose: Supports fast catalog exploration and reduces navigation detours before product evaluation.
observed_examples: NAMESAKE, Studio KARO, and Highsnobiety expose direct commerce browse paths within strong brand systems.
award_or_recognition: NAMESAKE and Studio KARO: CSSDA WOTD; Highsnobiety: Webby People's Voice winner
unsuitable_context: Deep catalogs where a second-level category choice is unavoidable.
accessibility_considerations: Full-cell native links support multiple input methods.
performance_considerations: Static links are fastest and most resilient.
recommended_application: Keep four direct href destinations visible at all breakpoints.

description: Make route location obvious before products appear.
user_or_business_purpose: Can reduce accidental re-selection and backtracking by clarifying what assortment is being viewed.
observed_examples: i-D and LNWY use strong location hierarchy within compact navigation; W3C supports programmatic current location.
award_or_recognition: i-D: Webby winner; LNWY: Awwwards HM; W3C technique
unsuitable_context: Single-page catalogs with no category state.
accessibility_considerations: Visual and programmatic current states must agree.
performance_considerations: No runtime cost.
recommended_application: Strengthen the current cell and preserve aria-current.

description: A small catalog-entry count can help shoppers choose a shelf before navigation.
user_or_business_purpose: Sets scope without promising inventory or availability.
observed_examples: Archive-led Because Recollection and Apple Music 100 make collection scale part of orientation.
award_or_recognition: Because Recollection: CSSDA WOTY; Apple Music 100: Webby nominee
suitable_context: Store shelves with materially different catalog sizes.
unsuitable_context: Counts likely to be confused with stock, or nearly identical counts that add no decision value.
accessibility_considerations: Label counts as items, never stock; keep them visually secondary.
performance_considerations: Build-time calculation only.
recommended_application: Prototype counts in the record-crate image, then remove them if they distract.

pattern_name: Cart remains a separate persistent utility
description: Do not merge cart state or checkout action into the category header.
user_or_business_purpose: Keeps browsing and purchase continuation available without competing inside one control.
observed_examples: NAMESAKE and other awarded commerce sites separate browse structure from bag utility.
award_or_recognition: NAMESAKE: CSSDA WOTD; Telepathic Instruments: Webby nominee
suitable_context: Existing BlackBox app shell with persistent cart.
unsuitable_context: A checkout page where cart summary is the primary task.
accessibility_considerations: Separate accessible names and focus order for category and cart regions.
performance_considerations: Avoids duplicating cart client state and hydration.
recommended_application: Leave StoreCartHeaderContainer ownership unchanged.
